mission.title: Install and Configure Kuberhealthy on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuberhealthy is a Kubernetes operator that runs synthetic checks as pods, allowing you to continuously verify that your applications and clusters are functioning as expected. It integrates seamlessly with Prometheus for monitoring and provides a simple JSON status page for easy access to check results.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Kuberhealthy
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Kuberhealthy to run in. This helps in organizing resources and managing permissions.

steps.title: Deploy Kuberhealthy
steps.description: Next, apply the Kuberhealthy manifests from the GitHub repository. This will deploy the necessary resources in the created namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: After deploying, check that the Kuberhealthy pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kuberhealthy will show the Kuberhealthy pods running in the `kuberhealthy` namespace, and you will be able to access the Kuberhealthy status page at `http://localhost:8080`. Additionally, the resource limits will be set as specified.

uninstall.title: Remove Kuberhealthy
uninstall.description: To uninstall Kuberhealthy, delete the resources created in the kuberhealthy namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kuberhealthy
upgrade.description: To upgrade Kuberhealthy, apply the new version of the manifests. Make sure to check the latest version on the GitHub releases page.

troubleshooting.title: Check Pod Status
troubleshooting.description: If pods are not running, check the status of the pods for any errors.

troubleshooting.title: View Logs
troubleshooting.description: To get more information about what might be going wrong, view the logs of the Kuberhealthy pods.

troubleshooting.title: Check Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the namespace has sufficient resources allocated and that there are no resource quota issues preventing pod creation.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have access to a Kubernetes cluster and the kubectl command-line tool installed and configured.
